commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2590a02f25224bb9b6da4d2c61e9f1903ac7e424)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces provider tool-schema object/array walks with guarded readers and adds tests for unreadable schema maps/arrays across OpenAI, Gemini, and DeepSeek compatibility hooks.

PR surface: Source +83, Tests +168. Total +251 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: a readable DeepSeek anyOf/oneOf array containing an unreadable object variant is preserved by recursive normalization and then spread during merge, which can still throw.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR is draft and maintainer-labeled, and the remaining action is for the author or maintainer to close the DeepSeek variant guard gap before merge.

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): guard provider tool schema walks
target.body:
## Summary
- Guard provider tool-schema compatibility walkers against unreadable plugin-owned schema objects and arrays.
- Preserve schemas during OpenAI/DeepSeek normalization when containers cannot be read instead of throwing or silently widening unions.
- Report unreadable Gemini/DeepSeek inspection maps as provider tool-schema diagnostics.

## Verification
- `node --import tsx --input-type=module` direct provider-hook repro: OpenAI normalize root, Gemini inspect properties, DeepSeek normalize root, and DeepSeek inspect properties no longer throw.
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/schema-normalization-runtime-contract.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (16 passed)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` found two real issues; both fixed. Final rerun clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_7a7ad5903ffb`, run `run_87d8e7265bfd`, exit 0

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: provider SDK tool-schema compatibility hooks can no longer crash when plugin-owned schema maps or arrays are unreadable during provider-specific normalization or inspection.
Real environment tested: local Codex worktree plus AWS Crabbox Linux remote changed gate.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/schema-normalization-runtime-contract.test.ts --reporter=verbose`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`
Evidence after fix: focused provider SDK schema normalization tests passed with 16 tests; direct repro returned/detected unreadable schemas instead of throwing; AWS Crabbox run `run_87d8e7265bfd` completed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0.
Observed result after fix: OpenAI/DeepSeek normalizers preserve unreadable schemas, Gemini/DeepSeek inspectors emit explicit unreadable-map diagnostics, and unreadable DeepSeek unions are not silently widened.
What was not tested: full repo-wide `pnpm check`/`pnpm test`.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
